intro: BTC holds near $64k with the fear/greed index at extreme fear after Warsh's hawkish FOMC debut; Grok 4.3 lands on Amazon Bedrock with a 1M-token context window at $1.25/M input; npm v12 ships in July with install-script blocking — every JavaScript CI pipeline needs an audit before then. AI Research quiet this week after last week's Fable 5 export-control saga.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 reaches Amazon Bedrock with 1M-token context and configurable reasoning
items.summary: xAI's Grok 4.3 reached general availability on Amazon Bedrock on June 16, giving AWS developers access to a 1-million-token context window and three reasoning effort levels (low, medium, high) via the standard Bedrock API. On-demand pricing is $1.25 per million input tokens and $2.50 per million output tokens; inference runs on Amazon's new Mantle engine with a 30,000-token maximum output per call.
items.why: AWS developers can now benchmark Grok 4.3 against Claude and other Bedrock models in a single API call — at pricing that undercuts the direct xAI API for most workloads, with no separate account required.

items.headline: Claude Code v2.1.181: /config prompt command, Bun 1.4 runtime, and mid-thinking auto-retry
items.summary: The June 17 Claude Code release adds /config key=value syntax to set any configuration value directly from the prompt — works in interactive, -p, and Remote Control modes. The bundled Bun runtime was upgraded to 1.4, long-paragraph streaming was improved to appear line-by-line, and API connections that drop mid-thinking now auto-retry instead of surfacing a raw error. Fixes land for JetBrains IDE flickering on 2026.1+ and Shift+non-ASCII key drops in Kitty-protocol terminals (WezTerm, Ghostty, kitty).
items.why: /config from inside the session eliminates the need to exit to settings for quick per-session overrides like toggling thinking mode or switching the active model.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 model card: configurable reasoning depth and Grok 5 at 10T parameters confirmed on roadmap
items.summary: Alongside its Bedrock debut, xAI published Grok 4.3's full model card: 1M-token context, 30K max output, and reasoning configurable at low, medium, or high effort — each level progressively more compute-intensive. The model is positioned for enterprise workloads including contract review, case-law research, and financial document Q&A. xAI separately confirmed Grok 5 is in training at an estimated 10 trillion parameters, with no public release timeline.
items.why: Per-call reasoning-effort control is now standard across Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok — making cost-per-task optimization a routine engineering decision rather than a model-selection one.

items.headline: npm v12 will block install scripts, Git dependencies, and remote URLs by default — July cutover deadline
items.summary: GitHub's June 9 changelog confirmed npm v12 will flip three historically permissive defaults: blocking preinstall/install/postinstall scripts from dependencies, Git-sourced packages, and remote-URL package sources. The change was accelerated by the Miasma supply chain attack of June 1, which compromised 89 packages across Red Hat's npm namespace in two hours. All three restrictions surface as warnings today in npm 11.16.0; v12 itself is due in July 2026.
items.why: Any CI pipeline relying on install scripts or Git-sourced dependencies will break silently on upgrade day unless teams run npm 11.16.0 now and clear the warning output.

items.headline: VS Code 1.124 enables Copilot Autopilot by default with a three-loop safety cap
items.summary: VS Code 1.124, released June 10, turns on Copilot Autopilot for all users by default — the agent now writes files, executes terminal commands, and responds to tool prompts without per-step confirmation. A new Advanced Autopilot mode uses a small utility model to determine when the task is genuinely complete rather than relying on fixed rules; autonomous loops are capped at three before Autopilot halts. Organizations can restrict the behavior via the chat.tools.global.autoApprove setting.
items.why: Autopilot on by default is a behavioral shift in the world's most popular editor; the three-loop cap and org-level policy gate offer meaningful guardrails for teams concerned about runaway agents.

items.headline: BTC stabilizes near $64k; Fear & Greed at 22 (extreme fear) as CME prices 61% odds of October rate hike
items.summary: Bitcoin has settled in the $63,500–$65,000 range in the 48 hours since the Federal Reserve's June 17 FOMC decision held rates at 3.50–3.75% but issued its most hawkish dot plot in two years — nine of eighteen officials projecting at least one hike before year-end. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index fell to 22, the lowest reading since February. CME FedWatch now prices a 61% chance of an October hike, effectively removing the dovish macro backstop that underpinned BTC's 2025 rally.
items.why: Chair Warsh's deliberate refusal to submit a dot projection and stripped forward-guidance language signal a genuinely unpredictable rate path — a structural headwind for all risk assets through at least Q3.

items.headline: Fidelity's FBTC takes $14M in inflows on June 17 as sector-wide Bitcoin ETF outflows total $111M
items.summary: Despite Bitcoin spot ETFs collectively shedding $111 million in net outflows on June 17, Fidelity's FBTC was the only major issuer to post positive flows on the day, attracting $14 million in fresh institutional inflows. Cumulative outflows over the past three weeks have exceeded $3.5 billion — the largest sustained withdrawal streak since the products launched in January 2024.
items.why: Selective accumulation at Fidelity while peers bleed suggests differentiated institutional conviction rather than a uniform exit — a pattern that has historically preceded stabilization.
